BENEFIT: Each admin request tied to user identity, full audit trail, no shared secrets

issues.name: update-subscription-payment-method

Handler:
- Add PUT /v1/me/subscriptions/payment-method endpoint
- Request: { payment_method_id: 'uuid' }
- Validate user owns the subscription (from JWT)
- Validate user owns the target payment method
- Validate payment method is active and NMI-backed
- Validate subscription is NMI-backed (not CCBill/Solana)
- Call NMI to update subscription's customer_vault_id
- Update local Subscription.PaymentMethodID in database
- Return updated subscription
Write tests for payment method swap flow

issues.name: unified-checkout-one-time-purchases
issues.description: Unified checkout endpoint that handles both subscriptions and one-time purchases based on Price.BillingCycleDays
steps: NMI Client:
- Add RunSale(vaultID, amount int64, currency, description string) to internal/integrations/nmi/nmi.go
- NMI API: type=sale, customer_vault_id=X, amount=Y, order_description=Z
- Return transaction_id on success
- Update AddRecurringSubscription to accept optional start_date parameter for delayed-start subscriptions
Unified Checkout Handler:
- Create POST /v1/me/checkout endpoint (or extend existing subscribe flow)
- Request: { price_id, payment_method_id } OR { price_id, payment_token }
- Look up Price and check BillingCycleDays:
- If BillingCycleDays != nil → subscription flow (see deduplication below)
- If BillingCycleDays == nil → one-time purchase flow
Deduplication Logic (CRITICAL - check BOTH subscriptions AND entitlements):
- Get user's active entitlements for the Product (from any source: subscription, one-off, admin)
- Get user's active subscriptions for the Product
- Determine 'coverage_end_date' = latest of (subscription.current_period_ends_at, entitlement.end_at)
- If coverage_end_date is far in future (not expiring soon) → Block: 'You already have active access'
- If coverage_end_date is soon (expiring within X days) → Allow purchase with delayed start
- If no active coverage → Allow immediate purchase
Processor-Specific Handling for Delayed Start:
- CCBill: BLOCK if user has existing coverage - CCBill API cannot schedule future start dates
- NMI subscription: Create with start_date = coverage_end_date, status=pending, charged on start_date
- Solana/one-off: Charge immediately, but create entitlement with start_at = coverage_end_date
NMI Subscription Flow:
- If immediate: create NMI subscription with no start_date (charges now)
- If delayed: create NMI subscription with start_date = coverage_end_date
- Store locally with status=pending until webhook confirms first charge
- Webhook handler activates subscription and grants entitlements
One-Off Purchase Flow (Solana, NMI RunSale):
- Charge user immediately (Solana tx or NMI sale)
- Create Payment record with purchased_at = now
- If delayed start needed:
- Create entitlement with start_at = coverage_end_date, end_at = start_at + duration_days
- Example: User has sub ending 12/20, buys 30-day pass on 12/05
- Payment: created 12/05, Entitlement: start_at=12/20, end_at=01/19
- If immediate start:
- Create entitlement with start_at = now, end_at = now + duration_days
Entitlement Duration for One-Time:
- Product.EntitlementsSpec has { entitlement_name: duration_days }
- Example: { 'premium': 30 } grants 30 days of premium
Cross-Processor Scenarios:
- User has NMI sub expiring → buys Solana one-off → Solana entitlement starts when NMI ends
- User has Solana entitlement expiring → creates NMI sub → NMI sub starts when Solana ends
- User has CCBill sub expiring → buys NMI sub → NMI starts when CCBill ends
- User has any coverage → tries CCBill → BLOCKED (CCBill limitation)
Goals:
- No gaps: new coverage starts exactly when old coverage ends
- No overlap: user doesn't get double time
- No double-charging: NMI delayed charges on start_date, Solana charges once for fixed duration
Pending Subscription Handling:
- User can cancel pending subscription before start_date → call NMI delete_subscription, remove record
- If card declines on start_date → webhook marks as past_due, dunning flow kicks in
Write tests for:
- One-time purchase (immediate)
- One-time purchase (delayed start due to existing coverage)
- NMI subscription (immediate)
- NMI subscription (delayed start due to existing coverage)
- CCBill blocked when existing coverage
- Cross-processor scenarios (Solana → NMI, NMI → Solana)
- Deduplication blocking (active coverage, not expiring)
Update frontend to use unified checkout endpoint

issues.name: cli-duplicate-detection
issues.description: CLI command to detect duplicate charges and multiple active subscriptions for the same user
steps: Create cmd/billing-cli/main.go with cobra CLI framework
Add 'duplicates' command with subcommands:
- 'billing-cli duplicates charges' - find users charged twice in same billing period
- Query payments grouped by user_id, price_id, month
- Flag any user with >1 successful payment for same price in same 30-day window
- Output: user_id, payment_ids, amounts, dates, recommendation (refund which one)
- 'billing-cli duplicates subscriptions' - find users with multiple active subscriptions
- Query subscriptions WHERE status='active' GROUP BY user_id HAVING count > 1
- Output: user_id, subscription_ids, prices, start_dates, recommendation (cancel which one)
Add --format flag for output (table, json, csv)
Add --dry-run flag (default) vs --fix flag for auto-remediation (with confirmation prompt)
Add date range filters (--since, --until) to limit search scope
Write tests for duplicate detection logic

issues.name: cli-orphan-entitlements
issues.description: CLI command to detect users with active entitlements that should have expired
steps: Add 'orphans' command to billing-cli:
- 'billing-cli orphans entitlements' - find users with premium but no valid source
Query logic:
- Get all users with active entitlements (end_at IS NULL OR end_at > now, revoked_at IS NULL)
- For each, check if they have a valid source:
- source_type='subscription' → verify subscription is still active and not expired
- source_type='one_off' → verify payment exists and end_at hasn't passed
- source_type='admin' → SKIP (intentionally granted, don't flag)
- Flag entitlements where source is invalid/expired but entitlement still active
Output columns:
- user_id, entitlement_id, entitlement_name, source_type, source_id
- source_status (e.g., 'subscription_cancelled', 'subscription_expired', 'payment_refunded')
- entitlement_start, expected_end, recommendation
Add --include-admin flag to also audit admin-granted entitlements (off by default)
Add --format flag (table, json, csv)
Add --fix flag to auto-revoke orphaned entitlements (with confirmation prompt)
Write tests for orphan detection logic

issues.name: admin-rate-limiting
issues.description: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints to limit blast radius of compromised JWT
steps: PROBLEM: If admin JWT is leaked, attacker could cancel/refund thousands of users before detection
Implement per-admin-user rate limiting using Redis (keyed by admin user ID from JWT)
Define rate limits for destructive operations: e.g., 5 cancellations/minute, 10/hour, 50/day
Define rate limits for bulk operations: e.g., 3 extend operations/minute
On rate limit exceeded: lock out admin for extended period (e.g., 1 hour) and alert
Add alerting/notification when rate limits are approached (e.g., 80% threshold)
Create admin rate limit middleware that wraps destructive endpoints
Allow super-admin or manual override to unlock rate-limited admin if legitimate
Log all rate limit events for security audit
BENEFIT: Limits blast radius - attacker can only affect ~5-10 users before getting locked out

issues.name: remove-wallet-verification-requirement

PROBLEM: This is an unnecessary step - the transaction signature itself proves wallet ownership
Update CreatePaymentIntent to accept any wallet address (not just verified ones)
Update ConfirmPaymentIntent to verify the signed transaction's fee payer matches the claimed wallet
Keep wallet linking endpoints for optional UX convenience (remember wallets) but don't require verification for payments

Remove isVerified checks from payment flows
Update tests to reflect new behavior (payments work without pre-verification)
BENEFIT: Simpler UX - users can pay immediately without linking wallet first

issues.name: solana-pay-transaction-request
issues.description: Implement Solana Pay Transaction Request spec (server-built transactions via HTTPS endpoint)
steps: CURRENT: We only support Transfer Requests (solana:<recipient>?amount=X) where wallet builds the transaction
TARGET: Also support Transaction Requests (solana:https://api.example.com/pay) where server builds the transaction
Create GET /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint returning { label, icon } per spec
Create POST /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint accepting { account } and returning { transaction, message? }
Server builds the transaction with the user's account as signer (not fee payer if we want sponsored txs)
Return base64-encoded serialized transaction for wallet to sign and broadcast
Update CreatePaymentIntentQR to optionally return transaction request URL instead of transfer request URL
Add config option to choose between transfer vs transaction request mode
BENEFIT: Enables complex transactions (swaps, multi-instruction), sponsored fees, and server-side transaction building
Write tests for transaction request flow

issues.name: solana-sponsored-transactions
issues.description: Allow merchant to pay Solana transaction fees so users don't need SOL in their wallet
steps: CURRENT: User is fee payer - must have SOL for transaction fees (~0.000005 SOL)
TARGET: Merchant pays fees - users can pay with just USDC/tokens, no SOL needed
Create separate 'fee payer' wallet (distinct from recipient wallet to limit blast radius)
Add fee_payer_wallet and fee_payer_private_key to Solana config (or use KMS reference)
Integrate with AWS KMS or GCP Cloud HSM for secure private key storage (never store raw key in config)
Update BuildPaymentTransaction to set merchant as fee payer (account[0])
Server signs transaction first with fee payer key, then returns partially-signed tx to user
User signs as token sender, transaction now has both signatures
Add rate limiting specifically for sponsored transactions to prevent abuse/draining
Add monitoring/alerting for fee payer wallet balance (alert when low)
Add config flag to enable/disable sponsored transactions (default off)
Consider using Helius or Jito for fee sponsorship as simpler alternative to self-managed keys
TRADEOFF: Adds complexity and security burden (private key on server) but improves UX significantly
Write tests for sponsored transaction flow

issues.name: webhook-lifecycle-entitlement-date-bug
issues.description: Webhook lifecycle tests fail due to PostgreSQL tstzrange constraint when entitlement start_at equals end_at
steps: [DONE] PROBLEM: PostgreSQL tstzrange(start_at, end_at, '[)') requires start_at < end_at, throws 'range lower bound must be less than or equal to range upper bound'
[DONE] ROOT CAUSE: Tests created entitlements at real time, but mock clock was set to past, causing end_at < start_at
[DONE] FIX 1: Updated entitlement service/repo to use injected mock clock instead of time.Now()
[DONE] FIX 2: Updated SubscriptionLifecycleService to propagate clock to EntitlementService instances
[DONE] FIX 3: Updated entitlement repo methods (EndActiveBySubscription, EndActiveByPayment, RevokeByID) to accept 'now' parameter
[DONE] FIX 4: Updated test helpers (CreateTestEntitlement, CreateTestSubscriptionWithOptions) to use suite.GetClock().Now()
[DONE] FIX 5: Updated webhook lifecycle tests to set mock clock BEFORE creating test data, then advance clock before revocation
[DONE] Added validation in entitlement Insert() to reject entitlements with end_at <= start_at
[DONE] Added validation in EndActiveBySubscription/Payment to error if revocation would create invalid date ranges
All affected tests now pass:
- TestWebhookSubscriptionLifecycle/Cancellation_cancels_subscription
- TestWebhookExpirationFlow/Expiration_cancels_subscription_and_revokes_entitlements
- TestWebhookChargebackTerminatesSubscription

issues.name: simplify-solana-pay-architecture
issues.description: Simplify Solana Pay to use Redis for pending state, server-side polling, and eliminate the SolanaPaymentIntent model

spec.overview: Replace the complex SolanaPaymentIntent state machine with a simpler architecture: Redis for ephemeral pending payments, a background goroutine that polls Solana every 500ms for confirmations, and Postgres only for confirmed payments.
current_problems: SolanaPaymentIntent table mirrors blockchain state unnecessarily
Multiple endpoints for what should be simple operations
Frontend polling triggers blockchain checks (fragile if frontend disconnects)

POST /v1/solana/pay.response: { url, reference, amount, token_amount, token, expires_at }
behavior: Generate random 32-byte reference (base58 encoded)
Calculate token amount from price using current quote
Build solana: Transfer Request URL with reference
Store in Redis HASH: solana_pay:{ref} → { user_id, price_id, amount, token, recipient, created_at } with 15min TTL
Add reference to Redis SET 'pending_solana_payments'
Return URL for frontend to display as QR
Check Postgres for Payment with this reference - if found, return confirmed + payment_id + signature
Check Redis for solana_pay:{ref} - if not found, return expired
Otherwise return pending (poller handles actual blockchain checking)

poller.name: SolanaPaymentPoller
poller.type: Goroutine with 500ms ticker (NOT a River job)
poller.why_not_river: River jobs have overhead, 500ms polling needs a lightweight goroutine
poller.startup: go StartSolanaPoller(ctx, state) in main.go alongside HTTP server
behavior: Ticker fires every 500ms
SMEMBERS pending_solana_payments → get all pending references
If empty, continue (SMEMBERS on empty set is ~0.1ms, negligible)
Batch RPC: send single HTTP request with N getSignaturesForAddress queries
For each reference with signature found:
- Fetch full transaction via getTransaction RPC
- Verify amount, recipient, and token match expected values from Redis HASH
- Create Payment record in Postgres (idempotent on signature)
- Grant entitlements to user
- SREM from pending_solana_payments SET
- DEL solana_pay:{ref} HASH
For each reference with no signature:
- Check if expired (created_at > 15min ago or HASH missing)
- If expired, SREM from SET and continue
- Otherwise, still pending, do nothing
poller.latency: ~500-900ms from user signing tx to confirmed in our system (400ms slot time + 0-500ms poll interval)

redis_structure.pending_solana_payments: SET of reference strings currently being polled
redis_structure.solana_pay:{reference}: HASH { user_id, price_id, amount, token, recipient, created_at } with 15min TTL

frontend_polling.description: Frontend polls GET /v1/solana/pay/status every 500ms for low-latency UX
flow: When status=confirmed → redirect to success page
frontend_polling.latency_analysis: Backend polls Solana every 500ms, frontend polls backend every 500ms. Worst case: user signs → 500ms until backend sees it → 500ms until frontend sees it = ~1 second total. Best case: ~0ms if polls align perfectly.

what_to_keep: Payment model and table (confirmed payments only)
SolanaTransaction model (audit trail of on-chain txs)
Transaction verification logic (amount, recipient, token checks)
benefits: Simpler mental model: Redis for pending, Postgres for confirmed
Server-side polling ensures payments processed even if frontend disconnects
Snappy UX: 500ms polling catches payments almost instantly
Efficient: batch RPC means 1 HTTP request regardless of pending count
Easier to reason about and debug

issues.name: uniform-mock-clock-usage
issues.description: Replace all time.Now() usages with injectable mock clock for consistent time-dependent testing
rationale: Currently only some services use mock clock (SubscriptionLifecycleService, EntitlementService, etc.)
Other services use time.Now() directly, causing timestamp mismatches in tests
Time-dependent logic (expiry, retry windows, period calculations) cannot be reliably tested
Edge cases like 'exactly at expiry time' are impossible to test deterministically

pattern.add_helper: func (s *Service) now() time.Time { if s.Clock != nil { return s.Clock.Now() }; return time.Now() }
pattern.propagate: Pass clock from runtime to services at startup
pattern.test_setup: Update SetMockClock() to set clock on all services

files_to_update.usages: Timestamp fields on billing events, 'now' for subscription/entitlement operations
PurchasedAt, CreatedAt, Timestamp on payments and events
UpdatedAt, StartedAt, CancelledAt, period end calculations
Payment intent expiry, entitlement checks with time comparison
IsPeriodExpired() comparisons - critical for subscription state
Calculating end time for admin-granted entitlements
Time for admin user lookup response

files_ok_to_skip.reason: Request timing for observability - not business logic
JWKS cache refresh - external auth infrastructure

issues.name: admin-grants-and-offline-purchases
issues.description: Design and implement admin-granted entitlements and offline/manual payments (cash, comps, partnerships)
questions_to_answer: What's the difference between an admin-granted entitlement and a one-off purchase?
Should admin grants create a Payment record or just an Entitlement?
How do we track the 'source' of an entitlement (subscription, one-off payment, admin grant, partnership)?
Should there be a 'reason' field for admin grants (comp, refund compensation, partnership, contest winner)?
How do offline payments (cash, check, wire) get recorded?
Do offline payments need a 'processor' value? (e.g., 'manual', 'cash', 'wire')
How does the CLI orphan-entitlements checker know an admin grant is intentional vs orphaned?

key_insight.problem: Current admin grant endpoint grants raw entitlement names - admin must know internal entitlement strings
key_insight.solution: Admin should grant PRODUCTS (or prices), and the system derives entitlements from Product.EntitlementsSpec
benefits: Admin doesn't need to know entitlement internals
If product's entitlements change, grants automatically use new spec
Consistent with how subscriptions and payments work
Audit trail shows 'granted Premium product' not 'granted premium_access entitlement'

current_state.admin_grant_entitlement: POST /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements grants entitlement with optional duration_days
current_state.no_payment_for_admin: Admin grants create Entitlement only, no Payment record
current_state.no_offline_payment_flow: No way to record a cash/manual payment that grants entitlements

entitlement_sources: subscription - linked to active subscription, revoked when subscription ends
payment - linked to one-off Payment record (online or offline)
admin - manually granted, optional reason, no payment linkage

offline_payment_endpoint.request: { user_id, price_id, amount, currency, payment_method: 'cash'|'wire'|'check'|'comp', notes }
behavior: Create Payment record with processor='manual', no external transaction_id
Grant entitlements from the Price's Product.EntitlementsSpec
Return payment_id for audit trail

admin_grant_vs_manual_payment.admin_grant: Quick entitlement grant without payment trail - for comps, bug compensation, testing
admin_grant_vs_manual_payment.manual_payment: Full payment record for offline money received - for cash, wire, partnerships with money

steps: Decide: should admin grants require a reason/category?
Add 'manual' as valid processor for offline payments
Create POST /v1/admin/payments/manual endpoint
Update Payment model to allow nil transaction_id for manual payments
Ensure entitlement SourceType correctly distinguishes subscription vs payment vs admin
Update CLI orphan checker to handle admin/manual sources correctly
Write tests for manual payment flow
Document when to use admin grant vs manual payment

issues.name: prorated-upgrades-downgrades
issues.description: Design proration logic for subscription upgrades and downgrades between products/tiers

upgrade.example: User on Basic ($5/mo, 15 days left) upgrades to Premium ($15/mo)
questions: Do we charge the price difference immediately? ($10 prorated for 15 days = $5)
Or do we charge full Premium price and credit unused Basic time?
Does the billing cycle reset or continue from original date?
What happens to entitlements? Immediate upgrade or wait for next cycle?

downgrade.example: User on Premium ($15/mo, 15 days left) downgrades to Basic ($5/mo)
questions: Does downgrade take effect immediately or at end of current period?
If immediate, do we credit/refund the price difference?
If end-of-period, do they keep Premium entitlements until then?
What's the UX? 'You'll be downgraded on [date]' vs immediate switch

mid_cycle_cancel_resubscribe.example: User cancels Premium, then resubscribes 10 days later to Basic
questions: Is this a 'downgrade' or a fresh subscription?
Do we credit any remaining time from cancelled subscription?
How do we track the relationship between old and new subscription?

ccbill.behavior: CCBill handles upgrades via Package Upgrade flow
ccbill.proration: CCBill may handle proration internally - need to verify
ccbill.our_role: We receive webhook and update entitlements accordingly

nmi.behavior: NMI is just a gateway - we control subscription logic
nmi.proration: We must calculate and charge proration ourselves
options: Cancel old subscription, create new one (simple but loses history)
Charge one-time proration amount + update recurring

solana.proration: Not applicable - each payment is independent
solana.upgrade_path: User just buys the higher tier product

stripe_model.upgrade: Charge prorated difference immediately, new price on next cycle
stripe_model.downgrade: Credit applied, new price on next cycle, keeps current access until then
stripe_model.billing_anchor: Can reset billing date or keep original

simple_model.upgrade: Immediate switch, charge full new price, credit remaining time
simple_model.downgrade: Takes effect at end of period, no refund

generous_model.upgrade: Immediate access to new tier, charge difference at next renewal
generous_model.downgrade: Keep current tier until period ends, then switch

upgrade: Option A: Immediately grant new tier entitlements, revoke old tier-specific ones
Option B: Keep both until period end, then switch
Recommendation: Immediate - user paid for upgrade, give it to them
downgrade: Option A: Immediate revocation of higher-tier entitlements
Option B: Keep higher tier until period ends (more user-friendly)
Recommendation: End-of-period - they paid for this period already

questions_to_decide: What proration model do we want? (Stripe-like, simple, generous)
Should upgrades be immediate or end-of-period?
Should downgrades be immediate or end-of-period?
Do we reset billing date on plan changes?
How do we handle partial refunds for downgrades (if at all)?
Do we need a 'pending_downgrade' state on subscriptions?
How does this interact with CCBill's built-in upgrade flow?
steps: Decide on proration model for our system
Document expected behavior for upgrade/downgrade scenarios
Implement proration calculation logic
Add upgrade/downgrade endpoints or extend existing subscribe flow
Handle NMI subscription updates (amount change, one-time charge)
Ensure CCBill upgrade webhooks work with our model
Update entitlement logic to handle mid-period tier changes
Add 'scheduled_change' field to Subscription for pending downgrades
Write tests for proration calculations
Write tests for upgrade/downgrade flows
